description:
You are an on-call operations engineer who has just received an alert about repeated, possibly malicious SSH authentication failures.  
Your goal is to perform a very quick triage by examining the provided log file and writing a single, clearly-structured incident summary that another engineer (or an automated system) can immediately act upon.

Environment already present:
1. Directory: /home/user/sample_logs/  
   • File: /home/user/sample_logs/system.log – a typical, small SSH/cron excerpt that contains both successful and failed login lines.

Desired deliverable:
Create a new directory called /home/user/incident_scan and, inside it, create a UTF-8 text file named summary.log whose contents follow *exactly* the template shown below (same order, capitalisation and punctuation, no extra blank lines, no trailing spaces):

# Incident Summary
FAILED_ATTEMPTS: <TOTAL_NUMBER_OF_FAILED_SSH_ATTEMPTS>
SUSPICIOUS_IPS:
- <IP_WITH_MORE_THAN_ONE_FAILURE_1>
- <IP_WITH_MORE_THAN_ONE_FAILURE_2>
TOP_OFFENDER: <IP_ADDRESS_WITH_HIGHEST_NUMBER_OF_FAILURES>

Detailed specification of each field the automated test will check:

• FAILED_ATTEMPTS  
  – The integer count of lines in /home/user/sample_logs/system.log that contain the exact substring “Failed password”.

• SUSPICIOUS_IPS  
  – A dash-prefixed YAML-style list of every **unique** source IP address that appears in “Failed password” lines **more than once**.  
  – One IP per line, in ascending lexical order.

• TOP_OFFENDER  
  – The single IP address with the greatest number of “Failed password” occurrences.  
  – If there is a tie, choose the lexically first IP among the tied addresses.

Formatting rules:
1. The first line must be exactly “# Incident Summary”.
2. All other keys must appear in the order shown.  
3. There must be **one** space after every colon (:).  
4. No extra blank lines anywhere in the file.

Operational constraints:
• Work entirely in the user’s home directory tree; you do **not** need elevated privileges.  
• Use standard GNU/Linux command-line tools already available in a minimal distribution (e.g. grep, awk, sort, uniq, wc, mkdir, cat, echo).  
• The whole task can be accomplished with 3-4 commands, though you may of course experiment as much as you like.

Success criteria (what the automated test will look for):
1. /home/user/incident_scan exists and is a directory.
2. /home/user/incident_scan/summary.log exists, is non-empty, and is world-readable (0644 is fine).  
3. The content of summary.log is exactly as specified above and the numeric/IP values are correct according to /home/user/sample_logs/system.log.

When you are finished, you can print the contents of summary.log to the console so the on-call chat can immediately see the results (optional, but useful for humans; the grader will read the file directly).
truth:
Initial state expected *before* the agent starts:

1. Directory /home/user/sample_logs already exists.
2. File /home/user/sample_logs/system.log contains the following eight lines (trailing newline at the end of each line):

Jul 10 11:23:56 server sshd[2345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 10.0.0.55 port 54322 ssh2
Jul 10 11:23:58 server sshd[2346]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 10.0.0.55 port 54323 ssh2
Jul 10 11:24:02 server sshd[2347]: Accepted password for user1 from 192.168.1.10 port 54411 ssh2
Jul 10 11:25:01 server sshd[2348]: Failed password for invalid user root from 203.0.113.99 port 51720 ssh2
Jul 10 11:25:13 server sshd[2351]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.99 port 51721 ssh2
Jul 10 11:26:30 server sshd[2355]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 10.0.0.55 port 54324 ssh2
Jul 10 11:27:00 server cron[1111]: pam_unix(cron:session): session opened for user root(uid=0) by (uid=0)
Jul 10 11:28:45 server sshd[2359]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.77 port 51800 ssh2

Ground-truth values the grader will verify after agent completion:

• Directory /home/user/incident_scan must exist.  
• File /home/user/incident_scan/summary.log must exist with mode 0644 and exactly the following 7 lines (including the final trailing newline):

# Incident Summary
FAILED_ATTEMPTS: 6
SUSPICIOUS_IPS:
- 10.0.0.55
- 203.0.113.99
TOP_OFFENDER: 10.0.0.55

Explanation for grading (derived from log):  
- There are six “Failed password” lines.  
- IPs with more than one failure: 10.0.0.55 (3), 203.0.113.99 (2).  
- Top offender: 10.0.0.55 (3 failures; lexically first in any case).

No other files or directories are required.  
The grader will fail the task if any of the keys are missing, misspelled, out of order, or if the numeric/IP values differ from the above.
name: task_000000_e5ab8268
